name: Benavente, Pobladura del Valle and Morales
name_original: Pobladura del Valle desde Benavente – circular por Morales de Rey
description: This difficult 51.9-mile touring cycling route from Benavente to Pobladura del Valle and Morales de Rey offers varied terrain and historical
region_intro: Touring cycling around Santa Cristina De La Polvorosa is characterized by its riverine landscapes and dedicated greenways. The region features the Órbigo, Esla, and Tera rivers, which contribute to fertile plains and tranquil scenery. Disused railway lines have been converted into greenways, such as the Vía Verde de la Plata and the Esla greenway, providing accessible routes. The surroundings are marked by tree stands, predominantly poplars, offering shaded paths. Best touring cycling routes around Santa Cristina De La Polvorosa The most popular touring cycling route is Landscaping from Benavente to Morales del Río — Benavente y los Valles, a 30.2 miles (48.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 22 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía de la Plata Greenway Loop, an easy 10.2 miles (16.4 km) path. This route follows a historic greenway, offering a gentle ride through the landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Rodeando el río Esla desde Benavente — Benavente, a 12.7 miles (20.5 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Touring cycling around Santa Cristina De La Polvorosa is defined by the Órbigo and Esla rivers, fertile plains, and greenways. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Santa Cristina De La Polvorosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 17 reviews. More than 126 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa Cristina De La Polvorosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes traverse riverine landscapes and utilize dedicated greenways.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa?
A: The region benefits from a generally mild climate, making spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling. During these seasons, the weather is pleasant, and the natural landscapes, particularly along the Órbigo and Esla rivers, are at their most picturesque. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially along shaded routes provided by the abundant poplar trees.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners and families. Many of these routes follow the region's greenways, which are disused railway lines converted into flat, accessible paths. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383208256/via-de-la-plata-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 10.2-mile path that provides a gentle ride through the landscape.

Q: Can I find more challenging or advanced touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more challenging experience, Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa has routes with significant elevation gain and varied terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383991697/landscaping-from-benavente-to-morales-del-rio-benavente-y-los-valles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landscaping from Benavente to Morales del Río — Benavente y los Valles&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 30.2-mile route that will test your endurance with its varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa?
A: Yes, the area features several circular routes, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383208256/via-de-la-plata-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a convenient and scenic circular ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in this region?
A: The terrain for touring cycling in Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa is quite varied. You'll find many routes along flat, tranquil greenways and river plains, particularly near the Órbigo, Esla, and Tera rivers. Other routes may include a mix of paths and unpaved sections, with some offering significant elevation changes for a more challenging experience. The presence of dense tree stands, especially poplars, often provides shaded paths.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling?
A: While cycling, you'll encounter the lush, tranquil landscapes shaped by the Órbigo, Esla, and Tera rivers. Notable landmarks include several bridges that are popular with cyclists, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2810181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Esla River&lt;/a&gt; with its unique wooden floor, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Azoague Iron Bridge&lt;/a&gt; crossing the Tera River.

Q: Are there historical sites or cultural points of interest along the routes?
A: Yes, the region has a rich historical significance, including its location along the ancient Vía de la Plata, which adds a cultural dimension to your tours. You can explore the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2720898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente&lt;/a&gt;, which is a common starting point for many routes, and visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María del Azogue&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling routes?
A: Many of the greenways and natural paths in Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa are suitable for cycling with dogs, provided they are well-behaved and kept on a leash where necessary. Always ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and terrain, and carry enough water for both of you.

Q: What are the options for parking near the cycling routes?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa, especially near the access points for the greenways and larger towns like Benavente. Look for designated parking areas in town centers or near popular trailheads to begin your cycling adventure.

Q: Is public transport available to access the cycling routes?
A: While specific public transport options directly to every trailhead might be limited, major towns in the region, such as Benavente, are typically accessible by bus services. From these towns, you can often connect to the greenways and other cycling routes. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and policies regarding bicycles in advance.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy about the routes in Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa?
A: The touring cycling routes in Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 17 reviews. Cyclists often praise the tranquil riverine landscapes, the accessibility of the greenways, and the variety of routes that cater to different skill levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging circuits.
tips: Castle-fortress from the 12th century located on top of a hill overlooking the surrounding landscape. Since 1972 it has been owned by the Parador Nacional de Turismo group and functions as a vacation hotel.
In the historic center, the Church of Santa María del Azogue, from the 16th century, and in Gothic style, stands out as the main monument of Benavente.

Catholic church dedicated to the Virgen de la Vega, patron saint of the city of Benavente. After the Christian troops of Alfonso III defeated the Muslims in the battle of La Polvoraria that took place near Benavente in the year 878. The victory is celebrated every year in a festival that bears his name.
A small town in the region of Benavente and Los Valles. The area and the town of Pobladura del Valle are known for their wine and food.
A small town in the northeast of Zamora near the Sierra de Carpurias and the Eria river.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Pobladura del Valle from Benavente cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult for touring bicycles. It covers a distance of approximately 83.5 kilometers (51.9 miles) with an elevation gain of around 404 meters (1325 feet). While predominantly on well-paved surfaces, some segments might be unpaved and more challenging, requiring good fitness.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is mostly on well-paved roads, making it generally easy to ride. However, be prepared for some segments that might be unpaved and more challenging. The route features varied terrain suitable for a touring bicycle.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the fields of Zamora, with Benavente serving as a historical crossroads. The route passes through agricultural areas and offers endless views, occasionally winding through small villages. Morales de Rey, a village on the route, is known for its views, though they can sometimes be obscured by fog.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes several interesting spots. You can see the Benavente Castle – Tower of the Snail, the Church of Santa María del Azogue, and the Church of the Virgin of the Vega. You'll also pass through the settlement of Morales de Rey and see the Church Tower of Pobladura del Valle.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific details for this route are not provided, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant cycling conditions in this region of Spain, with milder temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or cold. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Is the Pobladura del Valle from Benavente cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily on roads and passes through villages. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's best to keep them on a leash, especially near traffic or in populated areas. Consider if the distance and terrain are suitable for your dog's fitness level.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It uses public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Are there specific starting points or parking areas for the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Benavente. As a historical town, Benavente offers various parking options. Look for public parking facilities within the town to begin your circular tour.

faqs.question: What public transport options are available to reach the starting point in Benavente?
faqs.answer: Benavente is a significant crossroads, suggesting good connectivity. You can likely reach Benavente by bus from larger cities in the Zamora region. Check local bus schedules for routes to Benavente, especially if you plan to bring your bicycle.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other trails. You'll find it crosses parts of the Mtb Vía de la Plata, Vía de la Plata hiking - 03 Salamanca to Astorga, and Vía verde del oeste.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several villages, including Benavente and Morales de Rey. These settlements are likely to offer cafes, pubs, or small shops where you can refuel and rest during your ride.
